in [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich") is one of the city's four royal avenues next to the [Brienner Straße](/wiki/Brienner_Stra%C3%9Fe_(Munich) "Brienner Straße (Munich)"), the [Maximilianstraße](/wiki/Maximilianstra%C3%9Fe_(Munich) "Maximilianstraße (Munich)") and the [Prinzregentenstraße](/wiki/Prinzregentenstra%C3%9Fe_(Munich) "Prinzregentenstraße (Munich)").[[1]](#cite_note-1) The avenue is named after King [Ludwig I of Bavaria](/wiki/Ludwig_I_of_Bavaria "Ludwig I of Bavaria"). The city's grandest boulevard still maintains its architectural uniformity envisioned as a grand street "worthy the [kingdom](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Bavaria "Kingdom of Bavaria")" as requested by the king. The Ludwigstraße has served for state parades and funeral processions.

["Ludwigstraße München: Geschichte, Architektur und Sehenswürdigkeiten"](https://www.muenchen.travel/pois/stadt-viertel/ludwigstrasse). *Ludwigstraße München: Geschichte, Architektur und Sehenswürdigkeiten* (in German). Retrieved 2023-01-18.

[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [48°08′46″N 11°34′44″E﻿ / ﻿48.146°N 11.579°E﻿ / 48.146; 11.579](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Ludwigstra%C3%9Fe&params=48.146_N_11.579_E_source:dewiki)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Royal avenue in Munich, Bavaria

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/48/Ludwigstra%C3%9Fe%2C_M%C3%BCnchen.jpg/250px-Ludwigstra%C3%9Fe%2C_M%C3%BCnchen.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ludwigstra%C3%9Fe,_M%C3%BCnchen.jpg)

Ludwigstraße, Munich

The **Ludwigstraße** in [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich") is one of the city's four royal avenues next to the [Brienner Straße](/wiki/Brienner_Stra%C3%9Fe_(Munich) "Brienner Straße (Munich)"), the [Maximilianstraße](/wiki/Maximilianstra%C3%9Fe_(Munich) "Maximilianstraße (Munich)") and the [Prinzregentenstraße](/wiki/Prinzregentenstra%C3%9Fe_(Munich) "Prinzregentenstraße (Munich)").[[1]](#cite_note-1) The avenue is named after King [Ludwig I of Bavaria](/wiki/Ludwig_I_of_Bavaria "Ludwig I of Bavaria"). The city's grandest boulevard still maintains its architectural uniformity envisioned as a grand street "worthy the [kingdom](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Bavaria "Kingdom of Bavaria")" as requested by the king. The Ludwigstraße has served for state parades and funeral processions.

## Architecture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ludwigstra%C3%9Fe&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Architecture")]

The Municipality of the royal residence and capital city of Munich was first not enthusiastic about the extent of the new boulevard. The city authorities sought to impose a cut of the road, as they wrongly considered Munich not to grow in 100 years up to 1 km beyond the former city walls. Only when King Ludwig I threatened to transfer the residence to another city, the magistrate relented and approved the General Plan.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/ac/Aerial_image_of_Ludwigstrasse.jpg/250px-Aerial_image_of_Ludwigstrasse.jpg)](/wiki/File:Aerial_image_of_Ludwigstrasse.jpg)

Aerial image of the Ludwigstraße (in front of the Siegestor in the center of the image) and the Leopoldstraße (behind the Siegestor)

The avenue begins at [Odeonsplatz](/wiki/Odeonsplatz "Odeonsplatz") and runs from south to north, it leads from the [Feldherrnhalle](/wiki/Feldherrnhalle "Feldherrnhalle") in the south to the [Siegestor](/wiki/Siegestor "Siegestor") in the north, skirting the [Ludwig-Maximilians-Universität München](/wiki/Ludwig-Maximilians-Universit%C3%A4t_M%C3%BCnchen "Ludwig-Maximilians-Universität München") (LMU), the [St. Ludwig Church](/wiki/Ludwigskirche,_Munich "Ludwigskirche, Munich"), the Bayerische Staatsbibliothek ([Bavarian State Library](/wiki/Bavarian_State_Library "Bavarian State Library")) and other state ministries and palaces.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e1/Ludwigstra%C3%9FeMUC.jpg/250px-Ludwigstra%C3%9FeMUC.jpg)](/wiki/File:Ludwigstra%C3%9FeMUC.jpg)

View of the Ludwigstraße from above

The southern part of the avenue was constructed in [Italian Renaissance](/wiki/Italian_Renaissance_sculpture "Italian Renaissance sculpture") style by [Leo von Klenze](/wiki/Leo_von_Klenze "Leo von Klenze") from 1816 onwards.[[2]](#cite_note-2) Klenze's last building was the Royal Ministry of War (Ludwigstr 14, today Bavarian State Archives; 1824–1830). Starting with the Bavarian State Library the northern part was then constructed since 1827 in line with a plan of Klenze's rival [Friedrich von Gärtner](/wiki/Friedrich_von_G%C3%A4rtner "Friedrich von Gärtner"). The appearance is strongly influenced by Italian [romanesque architecture](/wiki/Romanesque_architecture "Romanesque architecture"), which developed a new architectural style, the 'arched style' (*[Rundbogenstil](/wiki/Rundbogenstil "Rundbogenstil")*).

Some buildings were constructed during the Third Reich, such as the Bavarian Department of Agriculture and the new construction which replaced the former [Herzog-Max-Palais](/wiki/Herzog-Max-Palais "Herzog-Max-Palais"). One of the best palaces of Klenze it was demolished and today houses the Bavarian head office of the [Deutsche Bundesbank](/wiki/Deutsche_Bundesbank "Deutsche Bundesbank"). After the World War II the opening in the middle of the Ludwigstraße for the new circular road Altstadtring caused further demolition and nowadays disturbs the original closed coverage type of the avenue which did not permit broad byroads.

Its extension north of the Siegestor in [Schwabing](/wiki/Schwabing "Schwabing") is called [Leopoldstraße](/wiki/Leopoldstra%C3%9Fe "Leopoldstraße"). At its entrance northwest of the Siegestor is the building of the [Academy of Fine Arts](/wiki/Academy_of_Fine_Arts,_Munich "Academy of Fine Arts, Munich"). In the distance further north in [Schwabing](/wiki/Schwabing "Schwabing"), the [Highlight Towers](/wiki/Highlight_Towers "Highlight Towers") are visible.

The U3 and U6 lines of the [Munich U-Bahn](/wiki/Munich_U-Bahn "Munich U-Bahn") run under the Ludwigstraße, with a [station](/wiki/Odeonsplatz_station "Odeonsplatz station") at [Odeonsplatz](/wiki/Odeonsplatz "Odeonsplatz") and a [station](/wiki/Universit%C3%A4t_station_(Munich_U-Bahn) "Universität station (Munich U-Bahn)") at [LMU Munich](/wiki/LMU_Munich "LMU Munich").

## Main sights

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ludwigstra%C3%9Fe&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Main sights")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3e/Munich_ludwigstra%C3%9Fe_1842.jpg/250px-Munich_ludwigstra%C3%9Fe_1842.jpg)](/wiki/File:Munich_ludwigstra%C3%9Fe_1842.jpg)

Ludwigstraße in 1842, state reception for Crown Princess [Marie of Prussia](/wiki/Marie_of_Prussia "Marie of Prussia")

*From south to north*

* [Odeonsplatz](/wiki/Odeonsplatz "Odeonsplatz") with the former [Odeon](/wiki/Odeon_(Munich) "Odeon (Munich)") and the [Palais Leuchtenberg](/wiki/Palais_Leuchtenberg "Palais Leuchtenberg")
* Bavarian Main Records Office (Bavarian Hauptstaatsarchiv, former [Bavarian Ministry of War](/wiki/Ministry_of_War_(Kingdom_of_Bavaria) "Ministry of War (Kingdom of Bavaria)"); Leo von Klenze, 1824–1830) (Ludwigstr. 14)
* [Bavarian State Library](/wiki/Bayerische_Staatsbibliothek "Bayerische Staatsbibliothek") (Bayerische Staatsbibliothek; Friedrich von Gärtner, 1832–1842) (Ludwigstr. 16)
* Bavarian Higher Administrative Court (Bayerischer Verwaltungsgerichtshof; former convent building, Friedrich von Gärtner, 1840–1843) (Ludwigstr. 23)
* Seminar Building of [LMU Munich](/wiki/LMU_Munich "LMU Munich") (Former Blindeninstitut; Friedrich von Gärtner, 1833–1825) (Ludwigstr. 25)
* Former headquarters of the BHS-Bayerische Berg-, Hütten- und Salzwerke AG (Friedrich von Gärtner, 1840–1843) (Ludwigstr. 27)
* [St. Ludwig University Church](/wiki/Ludwigskirche_(Munich) "Ludwigskirche (Munich)") (Universitätskirche St. Ludwig; Friedrich von Gärtner, 1829–1844)
* [Ducal Georgianum](/wiki/Ducal_Georgianum "Ducal Georgianum") (Friedrich von Gärtner, 1834–1841) (Professor-Huber-Platz 1)
* Max-Joseph-Stift (Friedrich von Gärtner, 1837–1840) (Professor-Huber-Platz 2)
* LMU Munich (Friedrich von Gärtner, 1835–1840) ([Geschwister-Scholl-Platz](/wiki/Geschwister-Scholl-Platz "Geschwister-Scholl-Platz") 1)
* [Siegestor](/wiki/Siegestor "Siegestor") (Friedrich von Gärtner, 1843–1852)

## References

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ludwigstra%C3%9Fe&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: References")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-1)** ["Ludwigstraße München: Geschichte, Architektur und Sehenswürdigkeiten"](https://www.muenchen.travel/pois/stadt-viertel/ludwigstrasse). *Ludwigstraße München: Geschichte, Architektur und Sehenswürdigkeiten* (in German). Retrieved 2023-01-18.
2. **[^](#cite_ref-2)** Mitev, Petar I. (2016). [*Identity & Building: Engaging in Dialogue with Context Through Architecture*](https://etd.ohiolink.edu/apexprod/rws_olink/r/1501/10?clear=10&p10_accession_num=ucin1459439638) (Thesis). University of Cincinnati.
